_comment: Generic English/business unigram LOG10-frequencies (log10 of relative frequency). DATA, not a .py dict (open-vocab rule). The glossary miner's distributional-anomaly signal computes a VALUE-using lift = corpus_log10freq - background_log10freq: a term whose in-corpus log10-frequency exceeds its general-usage value here by at least glossary.anomaly_min_lift is a coined/jargon candidate. A token absent from this table is assumed very rare in general use (the glossary.background_oov_logfreq floor), so a never-before-seen term recurring in-corpus lifts high and is mineable; a common word here has a high (close-to-zero) value that cancels its corpus frequency so it fails the gate naturally. Values are negative (rarer = more negative). Generated from a generic English+business word-frequency baseline; per-tenant override is a replacement file, never a code change.
